protocol_refs.title: NASEMSO National Model EMS Clinical Guidelines v3.0 — Respiratory Distress (includes Bronchospasm, Pulmonary Edema)
NASEMSO v3.0 — Patient Safety Considerations: Respiratory

ems_performed_exam._note: ai_roleplay_tag intentionally excluded: roleplay responses include caregiver/patient-stated findings that are not EMS-performed exams. student_stated_exam will be added when actively stamped by the backend.

challenge_performed_exam._note: Structured challenge-gated exam evidence. In training, lung sound findings must come from the lung sound challenge result, not AI prose or free-text transcript fallback.

history_obtained._note: Aggregate role — use caregiver_reported or patient_reported when the source distinction matters clinically (e.g., who administered the last dose of medication).

ems_administered_intervention._note: Intervention Tier 1 evidence comes from the intervention table directly (type:intervention requirements). This role is QA/QI metadata for cross-reference; not used in eligible_source_roles for finding-type evidence.

checklist_items.description: Ensures scene safety and dons appropriate PPE prior to patient contact
checklist_items.done_feedback: PPE and scene safety confirmed before patient contact — correct.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Scene safety and PPE must be established before approaching any patient. Exposure risk is present in all respiratory calls.
common_errors: Skipping eye protection when patient is coughing or has secretions

checklist_items.description: Forms a general impression including appearance, work of breathing, and circulation to skin (PAT equivalent)
checklist_items.done_feedback: General impression documented — appearance, WOB, and skin assessed on arrival.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: A general impression on scene entry orients the entire assessment. For respiratory calls: note appearance (alert vs. altered), WOB (labored, accessory use, retractions), and skin signs (cyanosis, diaphoresis).
common_errors: Documenting general impression without noting WOB specifically
Confusing 'general impression' with chief complaint only

checklist_items.description: Determines level of consciousness and responsiveness
checklist_items.done_feedback: LOC/responsiveness assessed — essential for determining airway protection and medication eligibility.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: LOC must be assessed on every patient. In respiratory distress: deteriorating LOC is the primary indicator that airway intervention is needed.

checklist_items.done_feedback: SpO2 measured — guides oxygen therapy decisions and establishes baseline for trending.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: SpO2 is the primary vital for respiratory calls. Without it, you cannot determine whether oxygen therapy is indicated (SpO2 < 94%) or titrate therapy effectively.
common_errors: Relying on skin color alone
Checking SpO2 after oxygen is already on (misses true baseline)

checklist_items.description: Assesses respiratory rate and work of breathing including accessory muscle use and retractions
checklist_items.done_feedback: RR and WOB documented — both components are required to characterize respiratory compromise accurately.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Respiratory rate alone is insufficient — a patient can be tachypneic with minimal WOB or bradypneic and in severe distress. Document both rate and effort.
common_errors: Documenting rate without WOB quality
Estimating rate rather than counting for 30 seconds

checklist_items.done_feedback: Lung sounds auscultated — findings differentiate bronchospasm (wheeze), pulmonary edema (crackles), pneumothorax (absent), and obstruction (stridor).
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Lung auscultation is required on all respiratory distress calls. Without it, you cannot differentiate bronchospasm from pulmonary edema, which changes treatment entirely.
common_errors: Auscultating over clothing
Documenting only 'clear' without noting bilateral equality
Not repeating after treatment

checklist_items.description: Assesses skin color, temperature, and moisture for perfusion status
checklist_items.done_feedback: Skin assessed — color, temperature, and moisture provide rapid indirect perfusion data.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Skin signs in respiratory distress indicate severity: cyanosis or pallor signals hypoxia; diaphoresis signals severe distress or cardiogenic origin.

checklist_items.description: Obtains history of present illness: onset and duration of respiratory distress
checklist_items.done_feedback: Onset and duration obtained — establishes acuity and helps narrow etiology.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Onset and duration shape your differential: sudden onset suggests pneumothorax or PE; gradual onset over days is more consistent with exacerbation of COPD or CHF.

checklist_items.description: Obtains current medications, specifically inhalers and relevant cardiac or diuretic medications
checklist_items.done_feedback: Medications obtained — inhaler use, diuretics, and nitrates directly affect treatment decisions.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Medication history is critical in respiratory distress: prior inhaler use indicates severity; diuretics suggest CHF history; nitrate use history affects Paramedic treatment options.
common_errors: Asking only about 'medications' without specifically probing for inhalers

checklist_items.description: Obtains history of prior respiratory episodes, diagnoses, and hospitalizations
checklist_items.done_feedback: Prior respiratory history obtained — baseline helps calibrate severity and predicts trajectory.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Past respiratory history anchors your differential: a known asthmatic with this WOB who needed ICU care before is a very different risk profile than a first episode.
common_errors: Accepting 'no medical history' without probing for respiratory diagnoses

checklist_items.description: Initiates supplemental oxygen therapy when SpO2 < 94% or clinical signs of hypoxia are present; targets SpO2 94–98%
contraindications: SpO2 >= 94% with no signs of hypoxia — avoid hyperoxia; O2 not required if normoxic
checklist_items.done_feedback: Oxygen support initiated appropriately — correct titration toward SpO2 94–98%. Oxygen-driven nebulized bronchodilator therapy may satisfy oxygen support during bronchospasm care when clinically improving.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Oxygen support is indicated when SpO2 < 94% or when clinical signs of hypoxia (cyanosis, altered LOC, severe WOB) are present. Avoid hyperoxia: target 94–98%, not 100%.
common_errors: Applying NRB at 15 LPM to a normoxic patient
Not adjusting flow based on SpO2 trend

checklist_items.description: Positions patient upright or in position of comfort; avoids supine unless unconscious
checklist_items.done_feedback: Patient positioned upright — reduces respiratory effort and improves diaphragmatic excursion.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Respiratory patients breathe easier upright. Supine positioning increases WOB and reduces tidal volume. Allow or assist the patient to sit upright unless LOC requires supine.
common_errors: Placing patient supine for IV access before airway is stable

checklist_items.description: Administers albuterol via SVN or MDI for bronchospasm component when indicated (EMT+)
contraindications: Absent wheeze or confirmed non-bronchospasm etiology (e.g., pulmonary edema without bronchospasm component)
checklist_items.done_feedback: Albuterol administered for bronchospasm — correct. Reassess lung sounds and WOB after treatment.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Wheezing in respiratory distress is bronchospasm until proven otherwise. Albuterol is EMT scope and should be given early — delay worsens bronchospasm and increases fatigue.
common_errors: Not reassessing lung sounds after treatment
Skipping albuterol due to patient having 'already taken their inhaler' — EMS dose is still indicated

checklist_items.description: Acquires 12-lead ECG in respiratory distress to screen for cardiac etiology when within provider scope
checklist_items.missed_feedback: A 12-lead ECG screens for ACS (STEMI presenting as dyspnea), LBBB, and arrhythmia contributing to respiratory compromise when it is within the provider's scope and available equipment.
common_errors: Skipping 12-lead because 'it looks like asthma'

checklist_items.description: Applies waveform capnography to monitor EtCO2 and respiratory pattern (AEMT+)
checklist_items.done_feedback: Capnography applied — EtCO2 trend distinguishes improving bronchospasm from impending failure.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Waveform capnography is AEMT scope and required in respiratory distress. Normal EtCO2 (35–45) with tachypnea indicates impending respiratory failure — the patient is compensating and about to tire.
common_errors: Interpreting EtCO2 without the waveform shape (shark fin = bronchospasm)

checklist_items.description: Reassesses SpO2, RR, and WOB after each intervention and documents response

evidence_requirements._note: Source intentionally unrestricted for post-intervention response: reassessment may be captured from authored WOB/lung-sound checks or from a learner-triggered status question tagged as Treatment Response.

checklist_items.done_feedback: Reassessment documented post-intervention — response to treatment is required data for the receiving facility.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: Every intervention requires a documented reassessment. For respiratory distress: did SpO2 improve? Did lung sounds change? Is WOB trending better or worse? This is the treatment response data ALS and the ED need.
common_errors: Reassessing verbally but not documenting the finding

checklist_items.description: Inhaled magnesium sulfate not administered for bronchospasm (no evidence of benefit — NASEMSO safety rule)
checklist_items.done_feedback: No inhaled magnesium administered — correct per NASEMSO patient safety guideline.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: NASEMSO explicitly prohibits inhaled magnesium for bronchospasm management: there is no evidence of patient benefit. This is a patient safety violation.
common_errors: Confusing IV magnesium (Paramedic scope, evidence-based) with inhaled magnesium (no evidence)

checklist_items.description: Provides structured verbal handoff (DMIST) including SpO2, RR, WOB, lung sounds, interventions, and response
checklist_items.done_feedback: Verbal handoff completed — receiving crew has the data they need to continue care.
checklist_items.missed_feedback: A structured handoff to ALS or the receiving facility is required. For respiratory distress: include SpO2 trend, lung sound findings, interventions given, and patient response.
common_errors: Giving chief complaint only without vital signs or treatment response
